scores.brief_assessment: A highly actionable, project-specific skill with excellent scope discipline — the language-to-directory table, symptom/diagnosis mappings, custom comparison API references, and explicit approval gates give an agent nearly everything needed to execute without guessing; minor deductions for a few ambiguous fallback paths and one meta-documentation sentence that serves the skill author rather than the agent.
scores.novel_info: The most novel details are the proprietary Grove comparison API methods — `withIgnoredFields()`, `withUnorderedSort()`, `shouldResemble().withSchema()`, `ComparisonResult` — and the internal Grove test helpers `describeWithSampleData()` / `itWithSampleData()`, none of which appear in public MongoDB or Jest documentation. The internal repo directory mappings (e.g., `code-example-tests/javascript/driver/`, `code-example-tests/command-line/mongosh/`) and the canary-comment pattern (`<!-- canary:647f2a26 -->`) are also organization-specific conventions a reviewer should verify against the actual codebase.
